title: Serpin production
abstract: Use of spray drying for increasing serpin expression in Bifidobacterium longum strain CNCM I-2618.
claims: Claims 1. Use of spray drying for increasing serpin expression in B. longum strain CNCM 1-2618. 2. A process for increasing serpin concentration in a preparation of B. longum strain CNCM 1-2618, wherein the process comprises spray drying the B. longum strain CNCM 1-2618. 3. A process according to claim 1 wherein the process comprises growing B. longum strain CNCM 1-2618 in a culture medium comprising a sugar selected from lactose, fructose and raffinose followed by spray drying the B. longum strain CNCM 1-2618. 4. A process according to claim 1 or 2 wherein the a culture medium comprising the sugar selected from lactose, fructose and raffinose at a concentration of 0.02 to 0.5 wt %, 0.05 to 0.15 wt %, 0.08 to 0.12 wt %, or about 0.1 %. 5. A composition comprising B. longum strain CNCM 1-2618 in the form of a spray dried powder produced according to the process of any one of claims 2 to 4, for use in the treatment or prevention of inflammatory bowel disease, celiac disease, non-celiac gluten sensitivity, gluten ataxia, dermatitis herpetiformis or wheat allergy. 6. A composition for use according to claim 5 wherein the composition is a food, a medical food, a tube feed, a nutritional supplement, or a pharmaceutical composition wherein the pharmaceutical composition comprises one or more pharmaceutically acceptable carriers, diluents and/or excipients. 7. A composition according to claim 5 wherein the food is selected from milk, yogurt, curd, cheese, fermented milks, milk based fermented products, rice based products, milk based powders, infant formulae and pet food.
